items.title: El Nińo Seen Tempering 2026 Hurricane Season, Curbing Risks to Natural Gas Market
items.summary: A developing El Nińo weather pattern could shape the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, potentially limiting storm activity even as ocean temperatures remain elevated, according to a new AccuWeather forecast.

items.title: Middle East War Takes Back Seat to Shoulder Season as U.S. Natural Gas Forwards Fall Again
items.summary: Bearish shoulder season fundamentals continued to overshadow war in the Middle East and its impacts on the global energy market as U.S. natural gas forward prices have declined since last week.

items.title: Spot Prices Wrap: Spring Weather Reset Sends Cash Natural Gas Lower
items.summary: Cash natural gas prices were mostly lower Friday in trading for Saturday–Monday delivery, as the market shrugged off weekend weather in favor of milder conditions ahead.

items.title: Futures Settle: Geopolitics Support Natural Gas Into Expiry, but Spring Fundamentals Weigh
items.summary: April Nymex natural gas futures closed out the prompt month higher Friday on position squaring and Iran war uncertainty, but it was unchanged week/week.

items.title: MidDay Snapshot: Northern Chills Fail to Prop Up Natural Gas Cash Prices
items.summary: Natural gas spot prices on Friday retreated in key regions as buyers looked past a chilly weekend and focused on forecasts for mild conditions ahead.

items.title: Permian Supply Pushes Mexico’s Natural Gas Imports Higher as Infrastructure Startups Near
items.summary: A surge in West Texas natural gas exports to Mexico has led to a ramp up in cross-border flows at the tail end of March.

items.title: TC Energy Reaches Key Agreements to Advance LNG Canada Expansion
items.summary: TC Energy Corp. has reached an agreement to develop the second phase of the Coastal GasLink (CGL) pipeline that would feed a proposed expansion of the LNG Canada facility in British Columbia.

items.title: MidDay Futures: Natural Gas Prices Top $3 as War Endures
items.summary: Natural gas futures advanced into midday trading on Friday as market participants balanced ongoing Iran war risks against softening domestic fundamentals.

items.title: Venture Global Adds LNG Cargoes to Europe in Edison Arbitration Settlement
items.summary: Venture Global Inc. has settled another contract dispute over the prolonged commissioning of its Calcasieu Pass LNG facility, agreeing to boost short-term LNG exports to Europe.

items.title: Midmorning Markets: LNG Exports a Bullish Bright Spot Despite Springtime Maintenance
items.summary: With weekly storage withdrawals likely in the rearview mirror for now and residential/commercial demand hitting the usual springtime doldrums, LNG exports are propping up overall Lower 48 demand in spite of seasonal maintenance and unplanned outages.

items.title: The Cushion Is Gone and the Oil Market Is Now Exposed
items.summary: The global oil market has been on a rollercoaster since late February, but the price reaction to the largest supply disruption in history has been relatively muted. The calm was not complacency; buffers were there to absorb the shock. But the system

items.title: Russia Warns of Force Majeure on Oil Cargoes After Port Disruptions
items.summary: Russia’s oil exporters are warning buyers that cargoes from its key Baltic ports may not be delivered at all. Russian producers are now saying they may declare force majeure on cargoes from key Baltic Sea ports after a sustained wave of Ukrainian dro

items.title: US Drillers Pull Back As WTI Soars Past $98
items.summary: The total number of active drilling rigs for oil and gas in the United States fell this week, according to new data that Baker Hughes published on Friday, bringing the total rig count in the US to 543, down 49 from this same time last year. The numbe

items.title: $100 Oil Is Solving Russia's Budget Problem
items.summary: Russia is getting an unexpected windfall from the war in the Middle East. The Kremlin’s oil revenues this month hit a four-year high as oil prices jumped to $100 per barrel amid the Iran war and the de facto closed Strait of Hormuz. Moscow expects so

items.title: U.K. Oil Producer Urges North Sea Revival as Hormuz Crisis Disrupts Supply
